body: I believe the CG solver is always valid (at least, we will find out when it is not). This addresses the issue discussed in #4719
title: Use CG as fallback solver for all cases

body:
Needed to complete https://github.com/CliMA/Oceananigans.jl/issues/3935

This PR allows us to dump grid data (and metadata) into a NetCDF and reconstruct the same grid from it later on:

```julia
ds = NCDataset(filename, "c")
write_grid_reconstruction_data!(ds, original_grid)
close(ds)

reconstructed_grid = reconstruct_grid_from_netcdf(filename)
original_grid == grid # true
```

It also adds tests for the grid reconstruction utilities that already existed, but which do not use NetCDF. (Namely, `constructor_arguments()`.)
title: Add grid reconstruction utilities to reconstruct Grids from NetCDF outputs
